summary: The diff implements a significant portion of the HMS requirements including Internet Identity auth, multi-tenant backend, CRUD for most entities, role-based dashboards, alerts, dark mode, search/filter tables, PDF export, and a medical theme. However, several specific acceptance criteria are missing or only partially implemented, particularly around backend-enforced multi-tenancy for all entity types, automatic stock deduction on treatment, doctor-scoped appointment filtering, patient appointment/prescribed medicines view, and images.

missing_requirements.requirement: Stock quantity updates automatically when a treatment record uses a medicine (REQ-4 AC)
missing_requirements.reason: The DoctorDashboard saves treatment notes locally but there is no call to a backend mutation that deducts medicine stock.

missing_requirements.requirement: Expose a query returning all medicines linked to a given supplier
missing_requirements.reason: No evidence of a getMedicinesBySupplier query being called or exposed. SuppliersPage only shows supplier CRUD with no medicines-by-supplier query.

missing_requirements.requirement: Doctors see only appointments assigned to them (backend-enforced)
missing_requirements.reason: The DoctorDashboard reads all appointments from localStorage filtered by hospitalId only, not by the doctor's principal. No backend query filters appointments to the calling doctor.

missing_requirements.requirement: Patients can create an appointment record linked to a doctorId and hospitalId via the backend
missing_requirements.reason: PatientDashboard books appointments entirely via localStorage, not via a backend canister call. Appointments are never persisted in Motoko stable storage.

missing_requirements.requirement: Super Admin report aggregates data across all hospitals; getMedicineStockReport, getBedOccupancyReport, getBloodStockReport backend queries
missing_requirements.reason: ReportsPage is only implemented for Hospital Admin scope. No Super Admin report page or cross-hospital aggregation query is visible in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: System-wide report summary cards are displayed on the Super Admin dashboard
missing_requirements.reason: SuperAdminDashboard shows hospital count and registered admin stats but no system-wide report summary cards for medicine stock, bed occupancy, or blood stock across all hospitals.

missing_requirements.requirement: Doctor availability calendar or slot picker is shown when booking
missing_requirements.reason: PatientDashboard shows a plain datetime input for booking, not a calendar or slot picker tied to doctor availability.

missing_requirements.requirement: Prescribed medicines list is visible per appointment
missing_requirements.reason: No prescribed medicines list is shown per appointment in the PatientDashboard.

missing_requirements.requirement: Alerts refresh when the user navigates between pages
missing_requirements.reason: AlertNotifications component fetches data on mount but there is no explicit refetch-on-route-change mechanism evident in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Generate hms-logo-icon.dim_128x128.png and hms-hero-illustration.dim_1200x600.png image files
missing_requirements.reason: No image files are present in the diff. The build request explicitly requires two image assets.

missing_requirements.requirement: Doctor records persisted in Motoko stable storage via backend calls
missing_requirements.reason: DoctorsPage manages doctors entirely via localStorage (hms-doctors key) rather than calling backend canister mutations.

missing_requirements.requirement: Hospital records persisted in Motoko stable storage via backend calls
missing_requirements.reason: SuperAdminDashboard stores hospital records in localStorage (hms-hospitals key) instead of calling backend canister mutations.

hallucinated_features.description: StaffPage: A full staff management module (Nurse, Receptionist, Lab Technician, etc.) with localStorage persistence
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not include a Staff entity in the data models (REQ-3) nor a Staff CRUD requirement beyond Doctors.
